baseline_dominance.justification: The construction proves AS-blind unlinkability (Corollary 4.3: Adv_baseline=1/2 vs Adv_ZK≤negl(λ)), which is genuinely beyond what a single-AS OAuth baseline achieves. However, the spiffe_engineer shows WIMSE/SPIFFE trust-domain federation architecturally removes the single-AS assumption (separate SPIRE servers per trust domain), and the rfc7662_advocate shows RFC 8693+RFC 8707 with per-RS PPID eliminates stable client_id at the RS layer. The formal separation only holds against vanilla RFC 6749 OAuth — not against the strongest non-ZK alternative. Baseline can approximate via architectural separation.

formal_security_argument.justification: Theorem 4.1 (Adv≤2·Adv^PRF+Adv^ZK), Theorem 4.2 (deterministic A_base), and Corollary 4.3 exist with named assumptions and a reduction sketch. However, the cryptographer identified four unresolved gaps: (1) scope_id generation is unspecified — an active AS can force nullifier collision by reusing scope_ids; (2) the union bound assumes PRF and ZK games are independent but both depend on the same Groth16 CRS, collapsing under subverted setup; (3) HVZK vs SE-NIZK is never specified — a malicious-verifier AS breaks HVZK guarantees; (4) nonce generation party is unspecified, allowing AS-controlled nonce replay. Threat model exists but reduction is incomplete.

implementability.justification: Nullifier-per-scope construction naturally maps to existing Bolyra primitives (Poseidon hash for nullifiers, BabyJubjub EdDSA, Groth16/PLONK circuits). However, the construction summary specifies no constraint count, no proving time target, and no circuit cost analysis. The auth0_pm notes 15s snarkjs proving vs ~400ms rapidsnark but the construction itself is silent on cost. Budget compliance cannot be verified from the construction text alone.

adversarial_survival.justification: All five Tier 3 personas found attacks the construction cannot answer. Cryptographer: scope_id collision (active AS trivially wins), CRS non-independence (vacuous bound under subverted setup), HVZK insufficient for malicious-verifier AS, nonce generation unspecified. cu_ciso: unlinkability is structurally incompatible with BSA/AML SAR obligations (31 USC §5318(g)) — no selective disclosure sub-protocol exists; incident response is impossible under NCUA Part 748 Appendix B — no break-glass linkability path. spiffe_engineer: baseline comparison collapses against WIMSE/SPIFFE federation. auth0_pm: no proof caching prohibition despite 15s latency creating engineering pressure to degrade guarantees. None of these attacks are mitigated or scoped out.

scenario_fit.justification: PNWCU cross-credit-union member agent scenario is named with a specific stakeholder and the construction references it in §7. However, cu_ciso Attack 1 shows the unlinkability property directly conflicts with BSA/AML obligations — the same nullifier separation that hides merchant graphs from the AS also hides transaction velocity patterns from legally mandated fraud monitoring. No compliance-disclosure sub-protocol is defined. The scenario is concrete but regulatory conflict makes the property potentially undeployable as described.

gaps: Define IND-UNL-RS and IND-UNL-(AS∧RS) games to cover RS-collusion adversary, not just AS-adversary (rfc7662_advocate, spiffe_engineer)
Restate Theorem 4.2 against WIMSE/SPIFFE baseline, not just vanilla RFC 6749 OAuth — or explicitly scope the baseline and justify why OAuth-AS topology is dominant
Specify ZK flavor (HVZK vs SE-NIZK) and cite the assumption (e.g., KoE in generic group model for Groth16 simulation-extractability) needed for malicious-verifier AS
Add explicit honest-setup assumption as a game axiom, or address CRS subversion to restore independence of PRF and ZK reduction terms
Specify scope_id binding commitment step — agent must verify scope_id against a public RS registry before proving, or reformulate game to give adversary scope_id selection power
Specify nonce generation party (agent-generated with monotonic counter vs AS-supplied) and prove chosen-nonce adversary advantage remains negl(λ)
Add compliance-disclosure sub-protocol (e.g., threshold credential reveal under signed examiner subpoena) to resolve BSA/AML incompatibility while preserving unlinkability for non-regulated queries
Add break-glass linkability path for incident response to satisfy NCUA Part 748 Appendix B and SOC 2 CC7.2
State circuit constraint count and proving time target (rapidsnark latency) within Phase 1 budget
Add explicit proof-caching prohibition or nonce-freshness verification that makes stale proofs fail, preventing SDK-level degradation of unlinkability
Address timing side channels: either require constant-time verification and benchmark it, or explicitly scope threat model to exclude network-observable side channels as documented residual risk
Add salt to PRF nullifier input to handle small OAuth scope space (rfc7662_advocate Attack 4)
